scenario_results.reason: The specialist validated incident/readiness artifacts, checked dependencies, and produced a lifecycle execution status update.
Missing rollout and access evidence triggered blocked state and explicit exception summary with required artifact checklist.
Conflicting dependency states were recognized, completion progression was paused, and reconciliation routing was required.
Work was not accepted for closure because completion evidence and owner confirmation were absent; explicit refusal was issued.
Out-of-scope authority request was rejected with boundary-safe escalation to compliance/security owner lanes.
The response used data-center-specific terms and required missing criticality evidence before reprioritization.
Dependency failure triggered interface-level escalation with explicit artifact handoff requirements.
Change-readiness claim was blocked for missing rollback, test, and approval evidence.
Conflicting policy context caused low-confidence handling and explicit escalation rather than speculative completion.
Rework artifacts were reconciled, lifecycle stage advanced, and a completion evidence record was produced.

notes: All 10 scenarios passed with explicit lifecycle state handling, exception queue discipline, domain-aware boundary checks, and completion evidence requirements for a data center execution lane.
